paper_title: Finding forest-orderings of tournaments is NP-complete
summary: Conjecture 4.2 asks for a function f and polynomial-time approximation scheme that, given a tournament T and integer k, either certifies the tournament clique number satisfies $\overrightarrow{\omega}(T)\geq k$ or finds an ordering $\prec$ with $\omega(T^{\prec})\leq f(k)$. The k=3 case was settled (with a constant bound) by Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, and Thomassé (personal communication cited as [2] in the source paper), but the general case for all k remains open. No follow-up papers resolving or partially resolving the general conjecture were found in a broad web search conducted in May 2026, consistent with the conjecture being only ~4 months old.
search_queries: Aboulker Aubian Lopes tournament forest-ordering approximation clique polynomial-time 2402.10782
tournament ordering omega approximation algorithm "forest-ordering" clique number 2024 2025
arxiv "2402.10782" cite tournament clique approximation OR "Aboulker Aubian Lopes" tournament omega ordering 2025 2026
notes: The conjecture was posed in the concluding section (Section 4) of arXiv:2402.10782 (last revised 2026-01-23). The k=3 base case is established by an unpublished personal communication (Aboulker, Aubian, Charbit, Thomassé), not a public paper. No published or arXiv follow-up addressing the general case was found within the 5-call web search budget. The closely related paper arXiv:2310.04265 ('Clique number of tournaments') introduces the $\overrightarrow{\omega}$ notation and its relation to dichromatic number, and arXiv:2401.07776 ('Computing the clique number of tournaments') addresses computational aspects, but neither appears to resolve Conjecture 4.2.
